pattern: Process columns left to right, deleting any column that would break an undecided adjacent-row order, and remember which row pairs are already strictly settled so future columns ignore them.
hints: Scan columns one at a time; the only rows that constrain a column are adjacent pairs not yet strictly ordered.
Once row i is strictly less than row i+1 at some kept column, that pair is settled and later columns never need to keep it sorted.
Keep a boolean per adjacent pair. For each column, if any unsettled pair has a strictly greater char above a lesser one, delete the column; otherwise keep it and mark pairs that became strictly less.

edge_inputs.description: an early strict order frees a later otherwise-bad column
single column, two rows out of order
ties must defer the decision to later columns

stress.description: random small-alphabet grids where many columns interact
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    cols = rng.randint(1, 8)
    alpha = 'abc'
    strs = [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(cols)) for _ in range(rows)]
    return [strs]
stress.note: tiny alphabet maximizes ties and forces deferred decisions
